back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land that I am giving you, the land shall keep a Sabbath to the Lord.
Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard, and you shall gather its produce.
But in the seventh year a Sabbath of complete rest shall be for the land—a Sabbath to the Lord. Your field you shall not sow, and your vineyard you shall not prune.
The aftergrowth of your harvest you shall not reap, and the grapes of your untended vines you shall not gather. A year of complete rest shall be for the land.
And the Sabbath of the land shall be for you food—for you, for your manservant, for your maidservant, for your hired worker, and for your resident who sojourns with you,
and for your livestock and for the wild animals that are in your land. All its produce shall be for eating.
And you shall count for yourselves seven Sabbaths of years—seven years, seven times—and the days of seven Sabbaths of years shall be for you forty-nine years.
And you shall sound the horn of blast in the seventh month, on the tenth of the month—on the Day of Atonement you shall sound the horn throughout your land.

And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty in the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a Jubilee for you, and you shall return, each to his property, and each to his clan you shall return.
A Jubilee is it; the fiftieth year shall be for you. You shall not sow, and you shall not reap its aftergrowth, and you shall not gather its untended vines.
For a Jubilee it is; holy shall it be for you. From the field you shall eat its produce.
In this Year of the Jubilee, you shall return each to his property.
And when you sell something to your neighbor or buy from the hand of your neighbor, you shall not wrong one another.
By the number of years after the Jubilee you shall buy from your neighbor; by the number of harvest years he shall sell to you.
According to the abundance of the years you shall increase its price, and according to the few years you shall decrease its price, for the number of harvests he is selling to you.
And you shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God, for I am the Lord your God.
And you shall do My statutes and My judgments you shall keep and do them, and you shall dwell on the land securely.
And the land shall yield its fruit, and you shall eat to satisfaction and dwell securely on it.
And when you say, 'What shall we eat in the seventh year? Behold, we shall not sow and we shall not gather our produce'—
then I will command My blessing for you in the sixth year, and it shall make the produce for the three years.
And you shall sow in the eighth year, and you shall eat of the old produce until the ninth year. Until its produce comes in, you shall eat the old.
And the land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; for you are foreigners and residents with Me.
And in all the land of your possession, redemption you shall give for the land.
When your brother becomes poor and sells some of his property, his nearest kinsman shall come and redeem what his brother sold.
And a man, when he has no redeemer for himself, but his hand prospers and he finds enough for his redemption,
he shall calculate the years of his sale and return the surplus to the man to whom he sold, and he shall return to his property.

But if his hand has not found enough to repay him, then what he sold shall remain in the hand of the buyer until the Year of the Jubilee. In the Jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his property.
And a man, when he sells a house of dwelling in a walled city, his redemption shall be until the completion of the year of his sale; days shall be his redemption.

And if it is not redeemed by the completion of a full year, then the house that is in the city that has a wall shall be permanently confirmed to the buyer throughout his generations. It shall not be released in the Jubilee.
But the houses of villages that have no walls around them shall be reckoned as the field of the land. Redemption shall be for them, and in the Jubilee they shall be released.
And as for the cities of the Levites, the houses of the cities of their possession—a permanent right of redemption shall be for the Levites.

And whoever redeems from the Levites—the sale of a house and of the city of his possession shall be released in the Jubilee, because the houses of the cities of the Levites—they are their possession in the midst of the sons of Israel.
But the field of pastureland of their cities shall not be sold, for it is a permanent possession for them.
And when your brother becomes poor and his hand falters with you, you shall sustain him as a foreigner and resident, and he shall live with you.
Do not take from him interest or profit, but fear your God, and your brother shall live with you.
Your silver you shall not give him at interest, and in profit you shall not give your food.
I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan, to be your God.
And when your brother becomes poor with you and sells himself to you, you shall not make him work the work of a slave.
As a hired worker, as a resident, he shall be with you. Until the Year of the Jubilee he shall work with you.
And he shall go out from you, he and his sons with him, and he shall return to his clan, and to the property of his fathers he shall return.
For they are My servants, whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. They shall not be sold as the sale of a slave.
You shall not rule over him harshly, but you shall fear your God.
And your slave and your slave woman who shall be yours—from the nations that are around you, from them you shall buy male and female slaves.
And also from the children of the residents who sojourn with you—from them you shall buy, and from their clans that are with you, who were born in your land—and they shall become for you as property.

And you shall make them an inheritance to your sons after you to inherit as property forever. Of them you may make slaves, but as for your brothers, the sons of Israel, a man over his brother—you shall not rule harshly.

And when the hand of a foreigner and resident with you prospers, and your brother becomes poor with him, and he sells himself to a foreigner-resident with you, or to a member of the clan of a foreigner—
after he has been sold, redemption shall be for him. One of his brothers may redeem him,
or his uncle, or his uncle's son may redeem him, or from his close relative of his clan, he may redeem him. Or if his hand prospers, he may redeem himself.

And he shall calculate with his buyer from the year he sold himself to him until the Year of the Jubilee. And the silver of his sale shall be by the number of years, like the days of a hired worker shall be with him.
If yet many in the years, according to them he shall repay his redemption from the silver of his purchase.
And if few remain in the years until the Year of the Jubilee, he shall calculate for him; according to his years, he shall repay his redemption.
As a hired worker year by year shall he be with him. He shall not rule over him harshly in your sight.
And if he is not redeemed in these ways, he shall go out in the Year of the Jubilee, he and his sons with him.
For to Me the sons of Israel are servants. They are My servants, whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
